title: xrMCP v0.1.0 Schema
description: Reusable schema library for xrMCP v0.1 documents. Consumers are expected to validate against specific definitions such as #/$defs/ToolManifest, #/$defs/ToolRegistration, and #/$defs/ToolBundle rather than against this root object directly.
$comment: The root schema is intentionally neutral. This file acts as a library of reusable definitions under $defs.

TemplateString.description: String value that may include template expressions such as {{ticketKey}} or {{config.baseUrl}}. Template substitution happens before request execution. Unresolved template variables are an error.

JsonPathExpression.description: JSONPath expression rooted at the response body. Example: $.fields.summary

OverridePath.description: Install-time override path rooted at config.* or execution.*. These paths identify values that may be supplied or replaced by the runtime during registration.

NetworkPermission.description: Allowed network destination expressed as a hostname, host:port, or host pattern. The runtime should interpret each entry as an allowlist item for outbound requests.

SecretPermission.description: Secret identifier the tool is allowed to reference at runtime, such as JIRA_TOKEN.

TemplatedValue.description: Primitive or structured value whose string leaves may include template expressions. Template substitution happens before request execution. Unresolved template variables are an error.

ToolManifest.description: xrMCP tool manifest document. In this repository, VS Code maps files matching *.xrmcp.json to this definition for authoring and validation.

outputSchema.description: Optional JSON Schema applied after any execution.response transformation. When execution.response is omitted or mode=raw, this schema describes the raw HTTP response body.

configSchema.description: Optional JSON Schema describing install-time or runtime configuration values. When ToolRegistration.config is provided, the runtime should validate it against this schema.

executions.description: List of executions to perform. At least one execution is required.

overridable.description: Install-time override paths that the runtime may supply or replace. Paths must be rooted at config.* or execution.*.

ApiExecution.description: Defines the structure of an API execution specification.

id.description: Unique identifier for the API execution specification. Should be a valid variable name, e.g. get_user_info. When outputMapper.mode is "map", every execution referenced in outputMapper.mapping[*].from must carry a unique id. This is not enforced by the schema but is a runtime requirement.

order.description: Execution order for the API. APIs with lower order values are executed first. Order values must be unique across APIs.

name.description: Human-readable name for the API execution specification.

description.description: Detailed description of the API execution specification.

Url.title: Url
Url.description: If object, contains the complete broken-down URL for this request. If string, contains the literal request URL.

raw.description: The string representation of the request URL, including the protocol, host, path, hash, query parameter(s) and path variable(s).

protocol.description: The protocol associated with the request, e.g: 'http'

host.title: Host
host.description: The host for the URL, e.g: api.yourdomain.com. Can be stored as a string or as an array of strings.

oneOf.description: The host, split into subdomain strings.
The complete path of the current url, broken down into segments. A segment could be a string, or a path variable.

port.description: The port number present in this URL. An empty value implies 80/443 depending on whether the protocol field contains http/https.

query.description: An array of QueryParams, which is the query string part of the URL, parsed into separate variables.

description.description: A description of the query parameter and its purpose.

variable.description: Supports path variables with the syntax `/path/:variableName/to/somewhere`. These variables are stored in this field.

Variable.title: Variable
Variable.description: Using variables in your xrMCP requests eliminates the need to duplicate requests. Variables can be defined, and referenced from any part of a request.

key.description: A variable key is a human friendly value that identifies the variable. In traditional terms, this would be a variable name.

value.description: The value that a variable holds. Ultimately, the variables will be replaced by this value when running a set of requests.

type.description: A variable may have multiple types. This field specifies the type of the variable.

AuthAttribute.title: AuthAttribute
AuthAttribute.description: Represents an attribute for any authorization method provided by xrMCP. For example `username` and `password` are set as auth attributes for Basic Authentication method.

Auth.title: Auth
Auth.description: Represents authentication helpers provided by xrMCP.

apikey.title: API Key Authentication
apikey.description: The attributes for API Key Authentication.

bearer.title: Bearer Token Authentication
bearer.description: The attributes for Bearer Token Authentication.

ProxyConfig.title: Proxy Config
ProxyConfig.description: Proxy configuration for outbound requests made by xrMCP for a particular URL match.

match.description: The URL match pattern for which the proxy config is defined.

tunnel.description: The tunneling details for the proxy config.

Certificate.title: Certificate
Certificate.description: A representation of an SSL certificate used for mutual TLS authentication.

name.description: A name for the certificate for user reference.

matches.description: A list of URL match pattern strings to identify URLs this certificate can be used for.

key.description: An object containing the path to the file containing the private key on the file system.

src.description: The path to the file containing the key for the certificate on the file system.

cert.description: An object containing the path to the certificate file on the file system.

src.description: The path to the certificate file on the file system.

key.description: The LHS of the HTTP Header, e.g `Content-Type` or `X-Custom-Header`.

Request.title: Request
Request.description: Defines the structure of an HTTP request.

anyOf.description: The standard HTTP method associated with this request.
A custom HTTP method associated with this request.

oneOf.description: The data contained in the request body.

mode.description: The type of data associated with this request.

contentType.description: Override the Content-Type header for this form data entity.
Override the Content-Type header for this form data entity.

OutputMappingEntry.description: Extracts a value from a specific execution's processed output using a JSONPath expression.

from.description: The id of the ApiExecution whose processed output is the source. Must match an ApiExecution.id value in the same manifest.

path.description: JSONPath expression applied to the named execution's processed output. Use $ to select the entire output.

OutputMapper.description: Defines how individual execution outputs are combined into the final tool output. mode=last (default) returns the processed output of the last execution by order. mode=merge shallow-merges all execution outputs in order (later executions overwrite keys). mode=map assembles the final output from explicit per-key extraction rules defined in mapping.

mapping.description: Required when mode=map. Each key in the output object maps to a source value extracted from a specific execution's processed output.

ResponseFilter.description: Filter stage for response processing. mode=all passes payload unchanged, mode=select keeps only selected fields, and mode=none produces empty output and skips mapper.

fields.description: Selector paths used only when mode=select. When the payload is a root-level array, prefix selectors with []. to project each element, e.g. [].id or [].owner.email. For object payloads, supported styles include name, owner.email, items[].sku, and items[].meta.tag. Maximum depth is 3 field segments.

ResponseMapper.description: Mapper stage for response processing. This stage remaps fields using JSONPath expressions.

allow_missing.description: When true, jsonpath expressions that fail to match any value will result in a null value for that key instead of an error. This is useful when mapping optional fields that may not be present in all executions. Default is true.

ResponseProcessor.description: Response processing configuration. Can have empty (no filter/mapper), one stage, or both stages. Example select+mapper: { "filter": {"mode": "select", "fields": ["name", "owner.email", "items[].sku"]}, "mapper": {"mode": "jsonpath", "mapping": {"projectName": "$.name"}} }. Example filter only: { "filter": {"mode": "all"} }. Example mapper only: { "mapper": {"mode": "jsonpath", "mapping": {...}} }. Example none: { "filter": {"mode": "none"} } (none means empty output and mapper is skipped).

network.description: Outbound network destinations this tool is permitted to contact. The runtime should treat these entries as an allowlist.

secrets.description: Secret identifiers this tool may reference during template substitution or authentication setup.

requiresApproval.description: Whether a human approval step is required before installation or execution, depending on runtime policy.

config.description: Runtime values injected into the tool, such as baseUrl or install-specific settings. When tool.configSchema is present, the runtime should validate this object against that schema before registration.

JsonSchema.description: Pragmatic JSON Schema subset used by xrMCP manifest fields. This definition is intentionally narrower than full draft 2020-12 but allows common schema keywords and extension keywords.
